project: Maple Street Medical Office Building (3 storey)

line_items.description: 5/8" Type X gypsum board, both faces
line_items.source_text: 09 29 00 5/8" Type X gypsum board, both faces 24,800 SF 1.65 40,920.00

exclusions.quote: this proposal EXCLUDES fire-stopping/firecaulking at rated assemblies
exclusions.risk: GC or another subcontractor must provide fire-stopping at rated assemblies. Confirm who is responsible to avoid scope gap.
GC or another subcontractor must provide scaffolding over 10 feet. Confirm who is responsible to avoid scope gap.
GC or another subcontractor must perform final cleaning. Confirm who is responsible to avoid scope gap.

exclusions.quote: EXCLUDES ... removal/haul-off of construction debris (by others).
exclusions.risk: GC or another subcontractor must remove and haul off construction debris. Confirm who is responsible to avoid scope gap.

assumptions.text: Pricing assumes building dried-in and conditioned prior to board installation.
One mobilization included; additional mobilizations billed at $850 each.
Pricing valid for 30 days from date above. Material escalation may apply thereafter.
Work is based on Architectural drawings dated 05/2026.

alternates.text: Add alternate ADD-1: Level 5 finish at lobby walls .......... ADD $6,200.00

scope_gaps.why: The quote includes 'Ceiling suspension grid framing' but does not mention the actual acoustic ceiling tiles or panels that would typically be installed within that grid. This is a common separation of scope.
Metal framing is included, but often wood blocking or backing is required for mounting fixtures, casework, or other items within or on framed walls. This is typically provided by a rough carpenter.
No demolition scope is mentioned. If this is a renovation project, demolition of existing walls/ceilings would be a separate scope item.
Permits and associated fees are not mentioned and are typically the responsibility of the General Contractor, but it's good to confirm.
While final cleaning is excluded, specific protection of existing or newly installed adjacent finishes during drywall work is not mentioned.

scope_gaps.missing: Specialty insulation (e.g., sound batts, rigid insulation)
scope_gaps.why: Only R-13 batts are specified. Any other types of insulation (e.g., sound attenuation batts, rigid insulation for exterior walls) would be a scope gap.
Access panels for mechanical, electrical, or plumbing systems are not mentioned and are often a separate line item or excluded from standard drywall scope.

notes: All required information was clearly legible and extracted with high confidence.
